title: citrus × bergamia subsp. mellarosa
intro: Citrus × bergamia subsp. mellarosa, also known as Bergamot, is a fragrant citrus plant belonging to the Rutaceae family, native to Italy and cultivated for its aromatic oil. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of bergamotene and limonene, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Bergamot oil has been used to treat various health issues, including anxiety, stress, and skin conditions, due to its calming and antiseptic effects, which can promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and improve overall well-being. Herbal preparations, such as essential oils, teas, and tinctures, are commonly used to harness the medicinal properties of Bergamot, offering a natural remedy for various health concerns.
